rationale: In the citing paper, Van Horen and Pieters (2012) is used not only in broad background lists but also to support a specific conceptual distinction: some copycats exploit positive associations without necessarily causing confusion. The authors then explicitly position their own contribution as different, focusing narrowly on confusion from attribute-based visual similarity in package design. This means the citation helps define the paper's scope and conceptual setup, even though its methods are not directly reused.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Van Horen and Pieters (2012) to represent an alternative copycat mechanism (subtle imitation for association transfer) and to delimit its own focus to visual confusion detection. The cited work therefore plays a substantive positioning role in framing what the new method addresses versus excludes.
